revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[ja:浅井神社 (高岡市石堤)]] {{cite|date=2022-7|qq=}} {{Infobox shrine | name = Asai Shrine | image = [[File:浅井神社境内.jpg|250px]]
Asai Shrine precincts | location = 3599 Ishizutsumi, {{ill|Takaoka, Toyama|ja|高岡市|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|39.3|N|136|56|15.2|E|region:JP-16|display=inline|qq=}} | map_type = Japan Toyama | deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mitsuhanome no Kami) | established = Yōrō 1 (717 CE) | type = Gōsha (District shrine) | other_names = Kawabata no Miya | festivals = Lion Dance of Asai Shrine |qq=}} [[File:浅井神社の社殿全景.jpg|thumb|Full view of Asai Shrine buildings]] [[File:浅井神社の獅子舞.jpg|thumb|Lion Dance of Asai Shrine]] '''Asai Shrine''' (浅井神社, Asai Jinja) is an ancient shrine located in the western part of Ishizutsumi, {{ill|Takaoka, Toyama|ja|高岡市|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Toyama Prefecture. The shrine sits on the left bank of the {{ill|Oyabe River|ja|小矢部川|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and is said to have been founded to pray for the protection and peace of the river, known for its flooding. The area was also an important transportation hub in ancient times on the Hokurikudō route, suggesting the shrine once enjoyed considerable prosperity. As the guardian deity of irrigation and agriculture, the shrine was believed to prevent flooding of the Oyabe River. It is also thought to ensure the flourishing of river transport on the Oyabe. Because the shrine hall was enshrined after building embankments in the area, it is also known as "Kawabata no Miya" (Shrine by the River). ==Worshipped deities (Saijin)== {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}|qq=}} ; Main deity * '''{{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|罔象女神|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Mitsuhanome no Kami) ==History== * 717 (Yōrō 1) – Shrine founded * 759 (Tenpyō-hōji 3) – Listed as "one tan of land belonging to Asai no Kami" in the map of lands donated to {{ill|Tōdai-ji|ja|東大寺|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in Etchū Province * 1881 (Meiji 14) – Ranked as a gōsha (district shrine), designated for offering of sacred food and cloth by the state ==Shikinai-sha dispute== This shrine is considered a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|延喜式内社|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tonami District|ja|礪波郡|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Etchū Province. However, some claim that the {{ill|Asai Shrine (Fukuoka-chō, Akamaru)|ja|浅井神社 (高岡市福岡町赤丸)|qq=|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, located in neighboring Akamaru Village, holds this title instead, leading to ongoing debate. Because the two shrines in Ishizutsumi and Akamaru are only about 300 meters apart, there is insufficient evidence to make a definitive judgment. ==External Links== * {{Official|http://www3.ic-net.or.jp/~asaijinjya/|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}} {{citation needed|Asai Shrine (Takaoka Ishizutsumi)|qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{citation needed|Asai Shrine (Takaoka Ishizutsumi)|qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} {{draft categories| |qq=}} [[ja:浅井神社 (高岡市石堤)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Asai Shrine (Takaoka Ishizutsumi) (Q115187945)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Asai Shrine (Takaoka Ishizutsumi)|lt=Asai Shrine (Takaoka Ishizutsumi)|ja|浅井神社 (高岡市石堤)|qid=Q115187945|1=Asai Shrine (Takaoka Ishizutsumi)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || — || {{ill|Asawino Shrine|lt=Asawino Shrine|qid=Q135040356|1=Asawino Shrine}} || {{coord|36.744250|136.937556|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182392 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q115187945}} [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:高岡市の神社]]
